steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: Lady Howard argues that "too rigidly sequestered" young people develop romantic, unrealistic views of the world, and urges Villars to let Evelina "see something of the world." The correct answer must reflect that Lady Howard thinks Villars is overprotective and that Evelina needs more real-world experience.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'shielding her from the influence of other young people': Lady Howard never mentions other young people; her concern is real-world exposure in general, not peer contact specifically. ✗
  • Choice B — 'mistrust has led him to be unduly wary of the Mirvans': Lady Howard does not accuse Villars of distrusting the Mirvans; she simply invites Evelina to join their trip and addresses his general over-protectiveness. ✗
  • Choice C — 'overly protective...benefit from a greater variety of experiences': Directly matches "too rigidly sequestered" (overprotective) and "it is time she should see something of the world" (more varied experiences). ✓
  • Choice D — 'imparted to Evelina his own idealistic view': Lady Howard says sequestration itself produces romantic fantasies, not that Villars personally transmitted his own idealism to her. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
